title: Northern Norway is ready to launch. EU Space Regulation — and its new Arctic policy – is not.
summary: Northern Norway is prepared for a space launch, but the EU's Space Regulation and updated Arctic policy are not yet aligned with this readiness. The European Commission is revising its Arctic policy, with a new statement expected this autumn, placing more emphasis on space-related aspects.

title: Shenzhou-23 crew arrives at Tiangong as China maps path to 2030 lunar landing
summary: Three Chinese astronauts arrived at the Tiangong space station, with one set to become China's first to stay in orbit for a full year. China also outlined plans for a crewed lunar landing by 2030.

title: NASA to add missions to SpaceX commercial crew contract
summary: NASA plans to extend SpaceX's commercial crew contract by adding more missions to ensure continued access to the International Space Station, as Boeing's spacecraft certification remains uncertain.

title: Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure
summary: Blue Origin has concluded the investigation into the New Glenn rocket's third flight failure, allowing the vehicle to resume launches. The company is now cleared to proceed with future missions.

title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the first Starship V3 on May 22, achieving most of its planned suborbital flight test objectives.

title: Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Viasat and SES a $437 million contract to build four small geostationary satellites for the Protected Tactical Satcom-Global program. This initiative aims to enhance military satellite communications.

title: NASA unveils sweeping reorganization
summary: NASA announced a major reorganization on May 22, combining mission directorates and reshuffling personnel. This marks one of the agency's most significant restructuring efforts in recent years.

title: Leveraging AUKUS and southern geography: building Australia’s dual-use space infrastructure for strategic resilience
summary: Australia is developing dual-use space infrastructure, including the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) Site 1 in Western Australia, to enhance strategic resilience in collaboration with AUKUS partners. The site aims for full operational capability by 2027.

title: SpaceX IPO filing casts Starlink Mobile as future wireless challenger
summary: SpaceX's IPO filing highlights Starlink Mobile as a potential competitor to terrestrial wireless networks, positioning it to offer services comparable to urban mobile networks. The document emphasizes Starlink's direct-to-smartphone capabilities beyond just remote-area backup.

title: Rocket Lab launches ninth Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its ninth radar imaging satellite for Synspective. The company also announced plans to sell up to $3 billion in stock to fund future initiatives.

title: Nothing beats a beautiful sunset (from space) | Space photo of the day for May 26, 2026
summary: A space photo of the day for May 26, 2026, showcases a beautiful sunset viewed from space. The image highlights the stunning visuals of Earth's atmosphere from an orbital perspective.

title: Scientists just found a supercharged supernova — powered up by a magnetic star corpse
summary: Scientists observed a supercharged supernova powered by a magnetar using NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray spacecraft. The explosion was unusually bright due to the magnetic star corpse created during the event.

title: Scientists want to send a roly-poly robot filled with 'dandelion drones' to investigate hidden tunnels on Mars
summary: Scientists propose sending a roly-poly robot equipped with 'dandelion drones' to explore hidden tunnels on Mars. This approach leverages biomimicry to enhance robotic exploration capabilities.

title: The original (and best) 'Transformers' movie is rolling back out into theaters for its 40th anniversary
summary: The original 'Transformers' movie is re-releasing in theaters for its 40th anniversary. Fans can relive the animated cult classic featuring Autobots and Decepticons.

title: Grab the Estes Blue Origin model rocket ahead of Memorial Day with it's lowest price since January!
summary: The Estes Blue Origin New Shepard model rocket is on sale for $29.99, its lowest price since January, available on Amazon and cheaper than the official Estes webstore.

title: Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel
summary: Lunar Strike presents a grim and realistic outlook on the future of space travel. The article suggests that space travel may face significant challenges and setbacks.

title: The most common type of planet in the galaxy may not look anything like Earth on the inside
summary: A new study suggests that the most common type of planet in the galaxy may not have a dense metallic core like Earth. Instead, their internal structures could be vastly different from what we traditionally assume.

title: China reveals 3-person Shenzhou 23 crew, including Hong Kong's 1st astronaut
summary: China has announced the three astronauts for the Shenzhou 23 mission, launching on May 24, which includes Hong Kong's first astronaut. This marks a significant milestone for Hong Kong's participation in China's space program.

title: 'Rick and Morty' Season 9: Release date & how to watch Adult Swim’s zany sci-fi satire
summary: Adult Swim announced the release date for 'Rick and Morty' Season 9, promising more zany sci-fi satire. The show will air on the network, continuing its tradition of absurd humor.

title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
summary: NASA is seeking creative partners to help tell the stories of its Artemis Moon missions, nuclear propulsion, and aeronautics through various mediums like film, music, and poetry. The agency issued a call for proposals on May 21 to collaborate with storytellers.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
summary: NASA will broadcast a live spacewalk conducted by two Roscosmos cosmonauts outside the International Space Station on May 27, starting at 10:15 a.m. EDT and lasting five hours. Coverage begins at 9:45 a.m. on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA’s YouTube channel.

title: Webb Studies Star Clusters
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope released a near-infrared image of a section of Messier 51's spiral arm on May 6, 2026, as part of a study of nearly 9,000 star clusters in four nearby galaxies. The data reveals that more massive star clusters form more frequently.

title: Keeping NASA Flying: Ground Crews Ensure Aircraft Readiness
summary: NASA relies on ground crews to maintain aircraft readiness for missions ranging from high-speed research to high-altitude science. At Armstrong Flight Research Center, specialized maintenance crews ensure the agency's aircraft operate safely and reliably.

title: NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management
summary: NASA plans to compete the next contract for managing the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), currently operated by Caltech since its inception. The move aims to ensure accountability and maximize value for U.S. taxpayers.

title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
summary: NASA announced an agencywide realignment to accelerate mission delivery and align with the National Space Policy. The changes aim to improve efficiency and speed in achieving national space objectives.

title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
summary: A new material discovered at NASA's Glenn Research Center could enable astronauts to melt Moon rocks and extract valuable resources like metals and oxygen for future lunar missions. This innovation supports NASA's goal of utilizing in-situ resources to reduce payload weight and sustain long-term exploration.

title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy cluster with galaxies of various shapes and sizes. The image also features foreground stars with diffraction spikes.

title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
summary: NASA will showcase a mission to boost the orbit of the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory using Northrop Grumman's Pegasus XL rocket and Katalyst's LINK robotic spacecraft. The event is scheduled for June 17 at NASA's Wallops Flight Facility in Virginia.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research on the ISS.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, at 5:30:21 pm CDT from an unspecified location. The mission impressed observers during its maiden flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled worldwide in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key advancement for future space missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is advancing planning for Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) tests.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. This follows multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by securing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company continued its growth trajectory from 2025.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers who contribute innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry. The image accompanying the text highlights this feature.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute system, referred to as a 'bake-out,' which is essential for ensuring the reliability of space missions. This process likely involves thermal testing to validate the parachute's performance under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its landing on the Red Planet after a 2028 launch.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and debris removal operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed critical space-like tests, confirming it is on schedule for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that current military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity due to high traffic. A new launch site is needed to accommodate future demand.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: The ESA Director General urges Europe to double its funding for space initiatives, emphasizing the need for greater investment despite recent increases. Critics argue that current funding levels remain insufficient for the industry's growth.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey identified improved Moon technology as the top priority for space tech investments. Two years ago, NASA sought input from the space community to guide its technology development focus.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: ESA has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research with cost-effective solutions. The missions demonstrate Europe's commitment to affordable scientific exploration in space.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO, expected next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers warn that the Golden Dome missile defense system could escalate the nuclear arms race rather than protect the U.S. The system is intended to serve as a defensive measure.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, potentially posing a security risk. The findings highlight concerns over collaborations with China in space-related projects.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews. This shift aims to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity in engineering production.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, has launched a new satellite bus business line focused on low-cost, high-power missions for communications, Earth observation, and national security. The move aims to cater to the growing demand for affordable and high-performance satellite solutions.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March to lead the company's ground technology advancements amid a rapidly changing market influenced by competitors like Starlink. Kuppanna emphasized the future of ground tech as cloud-native and interoperable.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched a new space domain awareness (SDA) software called Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract to build the first Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for a U.S. Space Force space domain awareness (SDA) mission. This marks Rocket Lab's first orders for GEO satellites.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat has successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, enabling messaging on Android smartphones via Viasat's L-band satellite technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its development through this financial boost.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to find innovative solutions to support satellite operations in orbit.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geospatial data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. This technology aims to enhance accessibility and usability of geospatial information.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance operational efficiency in airport operations.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial documents for an IPO, providing the first public glimpse into its financials. This marks a significant step toward the company going public.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: The upcoming World Radiocommunication Conference in 2027 (WRC-27) is expected to focus heavily on satellite-related agenda items, raising concerns among U.S. space operators about China's significant influence due to the conference being held in China.
